items.title: Rivian stock falls nearly 15% as company sells 75 million shares to raise capital
items.description: The capital raise occurred during extended hours trading following Rivian shares increasing 8.1% on Monday after increasing 19.2% last week.

items.title: Chip stocks sell off after Samsung earnings fall short of high AI bar
items.description: Samsung Electronics' results weren't enough to please investors after stock's 145% run up

items.title: Supreme Court's Kagan, Barrett to testify to House for budget request, a first since 2019
items.description: Supreme Court justices last testified to Congress was 2019, when Elena Kagan and Samuel Alito discussed a budget request with a House subcommittee.

items.title: Jim Cramer calls out buying opportunities in this 'vicious' market rotation
items.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

items.title: Don't rely on AI for personal finance advice, study finds
items.description: Researchers who studied generative AI platforms found that personal finance recommendations could be inconsistent or biased.

items.title: Trump renews Greenland threats at NATO summit, says U.S. could remove troops from Europe
items.description: NATO fell into a crisis earlier this year as Trump demanded that the U.S. must take control of Greenland on national security grounds.

items.title: Far more real estate agents now report seeing a balanced market, CNBC Housing Market Survey finds
items.description: Agents who reported at least one price cut to active listings dropped dramatically from prior surveys.

items.title: Buy these quality, low-stress stocks for the summer, says Jefferies
items.description: This summer, Jefferies recommends focusing on some high-quality stocks that have been left behind by the AI momentum trade.

items.title: Stellantis to sell small Fiat Topolino EV for $13,995 in U.S.
items.description: While the Topolino resembles a small car such as the Fiat 500, the EV is actually a quadricycle that functions more like a golf cart.

items.title: Billionaires John and Laura Arnold commit $2.6 million to study online sports betting risk
items.description: John Arnold and Laura Arnold, who run Arnold Ventures, are investing $2.6 million for university research into potential harms of online sports betting.

items.title: Amazon raising at least $25 billion in bond sale, won't issue more debt in 2026
items.description: It marks Amazon's latest debt raise as it looks to buttress its massive investments in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Trump ally Nigel Farage quits UK parliament amid finance scandal to fight special election
items.description: Reform U.K. leader Nigel Farage has quit as MP for Clacton, forcing a by-election that he has said he will fight.

items.title: Trump arrives in Turkey as NATO is strained by Russian attacks, U.S. impatience
items.description: Trump frequently vented about NATO members' refusal to heed U.S. calls for help clearing the Strait of Hormuz during its campaign against Iran.

items.title: Chinese lidar maker with Nvidia ties accused of being cyber risk for U.S.
items.description: Hesai Technology was blacklisted as a national security threat in 2024 by the U.S. Department of Defense, which designated Hesai as a Chinese military entity.

items.title: Why oil investors fear the next toll fight could be the Strait of Malacca
items.description: The prospect of fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz has sparked alarm, not least by investors who fear it could be replicated in other maritime corridors.

items.title: World Cup in photos: Belgium beats U.S. 4-1 after Balogun controversy, Ronaldo exits
items.description: The U.S. is out of the World Cup, losing 4-1 to Belgium in the Round of 16 in a match blemished by suspension of Folarin Balogun's red card. Ronaldo exits.

items.title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with U.S. companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
items.description: Recent model releases from Chinese companies including DeepSeek and Z.ai are seen by many as highly competitive compared to leading U.S. frontier systems.

items.title: Google backs nuclear fusion startup targeting Europe’s first commercial power plant
items.description: Proxima Fusion has raised $468 million as it looks to move towards commercializing the promising but infamously difficult technical challenge of nuclear fusion.

items.title: India fashion retailer Trent tumbles 11% as revenue growth disappoints
items.description: Shares of one of India's biggest fashion retailers, Trent, fell over 10% after the company reported weak earnings growth.

items.title: Hanwha Ocean shares sink 23% as it loses bid to build Canada's next fleet of submarines
items.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Monday that Germany's Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems would be the preferred supplier for delivering the submarines.
